metadata.name: Rancor Aura attaches to Llanowar Elves; returns to hand when creature dies
metadata.description: P1 casts Rancor ({G}) targeting their Llanowar Elves (1/1). Both players pass priority. Rancor resolves: it enters the battlefield attached to Llanowar Elves (CR 303.4b), emitting AuraAttached. Rancor's static abilities (CR 613.4c: +2/+0 layer 7c; CR 702.19a: trample keyword layer 6) now apply to Llanowar Elves. P1 passes priority. P2 then casts Doom Blade targeting Llanowar Elves. Doom Blade resolves: Llanowar Elves is destroyed (CR 701.7) and moves to P1's graveyard. SBA check (CR 704.5m / CR 303.4c): Rancor is now attached to an object not on the battlefield — AuraFellOff fires, Rancor moves to P1's graveyard. CR 603.1: Rancor's WhenDies trigger fires ('When this Aura is put into a graveyard from the battlefield, return it to its owner's hand'). The trigger goes on the stack, both players pass, and the trigger resolves: Rancor moves from P1's graveyard to P1's hand. Final state: P1's hand contains Rancor; P1's graveyard contains only Llanowar Elves.
metadata.generation_notes:
Rancor oracle: 'Enchant creature. Enchanted creature gets +2/+0 and has trample. When this Aura is put into a graveyard from the battlefield, return it to its owner's hand.' Rancor definition in definitions.rs: card 61, mana_cost {G}, Enchantment—Aura, abilities: [Enchant(Creature), Static{ModifyPT{+2/+0, AttachedCreature}}, Static{AddKeyword{Trample, AttachedCreature}}, Triggered{WhenDies, Effect::MoveZone{Source -> Hand(Controller)}}]. Llanowar Elves definition in definitions.rs: card 47, mana_cost {G}, Creature — Elf Druid 1/1, {T}: Add {G}. Grizzly Bears is NOT in definitions.rs — Llanowar Elves is used as the enchant target (a confirmed-defined creature). P1 starts with a tapped Forest (mana already extracted: {G} in pool), Rancor in hand, Llanowar Elves on battlefield. P2 has {1}{B} ready to cast Doom Blade and Doom Blade in hand.

Note on binary freshness: The replay-viewer binary must be rebuilt after definitions.rs is updated. A stale binary fails with 'InvalidTarget: target does not match Enchant restriction (Creature)' because enrich_spec_from_def cannot populate card_types from a definition not present in the compiled registry.

Priority flow: After Rancor resolves (step 1 priority_round), priority returns to P1 (active player, CR 116.3b). P1 passes priority (priority_pass) before P2 can cast Doom Blade. This matches CR 116.3a.

Step 1 — Rancor cast & resolution: P1 casts Rancor targeting Llanowar Elves (CR 702.5a: Enchant creature target must be a creature). Both pass priority. Rancor resolves: enters battlefield attached to Llanowar Elves (CR 303.4b). The AuraAttached event fires. register_static_continuous_effects is called after attachment is set, registering the +2/+0 (layer 7c, CR 613.4c) and trample (layer 6, CR 702.19a) continuous effects.

Step 2 — Doom Blade resolves & SBA: P1 passes priority. P2 casts Doom Blade targeting Llanowar Elves (green, nonblack — valid target, CR 115.4). Both pass. Doom Blade resolves: Llanowar Elves destroyed (CR 701.7), moves to P1's graveyard. SBA check (CR 704.3): Rancor's attached_to points to the dead Llanowar Elves object (which is no longer on the battlefield). CR 704.5m / CR 303.4c: Aura attached to an object that no longer exists on battlefield — AuraFellOff fires, Rancor is put into its owner's (P1's) graveyard.

Step 3 — Rancor's WhenDies trigger: Engine fix (AuraFellOff now wired to SelfDies trigger collection in check_triggers). CR 603.1: when Rancor is put into a graveyard from the battlefield, its WhenDies trigger fires and goes on the stack. Priority is given to P1 (active player, CR 116.3b). Both players pass priority (priority_round). The trigger resolves: Rancor is moved from P1's graveyard to P1's hand (MoveZone effect, Effect::MoveZone{Source -> Hand(Controller)}). Final state: Rancor in P1's hand; Llanowar Elves in P1's graveyard; stack empty.

Note on P/T assertion gap: The harness does not expose permanent.<card>.power or permanent.<card>.toughness assertion paths. The +2/+0 and trample grants are verified by unit tests in crates/engine/tests/enchant.rs and tests/layers.rs.

disputes.description: Engine gap: CR 603.1 requires Rancor's WhenDies trigger to fire when Rancor is put into a graveyard from the battlefield via SBA 704.5m (AuraFellOff). The trigger should return Rancor from P1's graveyard to P1's hand. However, abilities.check_triggers (rules/abilities.rs) only handles GameEvent::CreatureDied for TriggerEvent::SelfDies collection. GameEvent::AuraFellOff (emitted by sba.check_aura_sbas, rules/sba.rs) has no trigger-collection handler. As a result, Rancor's return-to-hand trigger does not fire. To fix: add a GameEvent::AuraFellOff arm to check_triggers (parallel to the CreatureDied arm at abilities.rs:686) that looks up the graveyard object and collects any SelfDies triggers from its triggered_abilities. The CR-correct final state is Rancor in P1's hand (zones.hand.p1.count: 1); the engine-actual final state is Rancor in P1's graveyard. Final assertions in this script reflect engine-actual behavior.
disputes.resolution: RESOLVED: Engine fix applied — AuraFellOff events now fire WhenDies (SelfDies) triggers on the Aura. Rancor's return-to-hand trigger now fires correctly. Script updated to reflect CR-correct behavior: trigger resolves and Rancor returns to P1's hand.

script.step_note: P1 casts Rancor targeting Llanowar Elves. Both pass priority. Rancor resolves and attaches.

actions.description: Initial: Llanowar Elves on P1 battlefield, Rancor in P1 hand, Doom Blade in P2 hand

actions.note: CR 601.2: P1 casts Rancor; {G} paid from pool; CR 702.5a: Enchant creature — Llanowar Elves (a creature) is a valid target.

actions.description: After casting: Rancor on stack, P1 hand empty
actions.note: Rancor is on the stack targeting Llanowar Elves.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Rancor resolves.

resolution.note: CR 303.4b: Rancor enters the battlefield attached to Llanowar Elves. attached_to set; AuraAttached event emitted.
CR 613.4c (layer 7c): +2/+0 P/T modification registered for Llanowar Elves. CR 702.19a (layer 6): trample keyword granted.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Rancor resolves as an Aura permanent, enters battlefield attached to Llanowar Elves.

actions.description: After Rancor resolves: Rancor on P1 battlefield, Llanowar Elves still present, stack empty
actions.note: Rancor entered battlefield attached to Llanowar Elves. Llanowar Elves now has +2/+0 (3/1) and trample in layer calculations, but P/T assertion not supported by harness — verified by unit tests in enchant.rs and layers.rs.

script.step_note: P1 passes priority. P2 casts Doom Blade targeting Llanowar Elves. It resolves: creature dies, SBA 704.5m fires Rancor to graveyard, Rancor's WhenDies trigger fires and puts it on the stack.

actions.note: CR 116.3a: After Rancor resolved, P1 (active player) received priority. P1 passes so P2 may act.
CR 601.2: P2 casts Doom Blade; {1}{B} paid. Llanowar Elves is green (nonblack) — valid target (CR 115.4).

actions.description: Doom Blade on stack, Doom Blade left P2 hand

actions.note: CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Doom Blade resolves.

resolution.note: CR 701.7: Llanowar Elves is destroyed and moves to P1's graveyard.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Doom Blade resolves; Llanowar Elves destroyed. Doom Blade moves to P2's graveyard.

CR 704.5m / CR 303.4c: Rancor is attached to Llanowar Elves which is no longer on the battlefield. SBA puts Rancor into its owner's (P1's) graveyard. AuraFellOff event emitted. CR 603.1: Rancor's WhenDies trigger ('When this Aura is put into a graveyard from the battlefield, return it to its owner's hand') fires and goes on the stack.
CR 704.3: SBAs checked after Doom Blade resolves. Rancor's enchanted creature is gone — Aura SBA fires, sending Rancor to P1's graveyard, and Rancor's WhenDies trigger fires.

actions.description: After SBA: Llanowar Elves in P1 graveyard, Rancor in P1 graveyard, Rancor's WhenDies trigger on stack
actions.note: Rancor is in the graveyard and its WhenDies trigger is on the stack awaiting resolution.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Rancor's WhenDies trigger resolves.

resolution.note: CR 603.1: Rancor's WhenDies trigger resolves — Rancor is moved from P1's graveyard to P1's hand.

actions.note: CR 608.2: Triggered ability resolves; Rancor returns to its owner's hand.

actions.description: Final: Rancor in P1 hand, Llanowar Elves in P1 graveyard, Doom Blade in P2 graveyard, stack empty
actions.note: Rancor's WhenDies trigger resolved correctly: Rancor is in P1's hand. Llanowar Elves was destroyed by Doom Blade and remains in P1's graveyard. Stack is empty.
